story: Lyra comes to kill Jasper Waldegrave on Ironport’s moonlit beach. Rook appears, grinning, admits he drugged Jasper and is on the same contract. He lifts a signet from Jasper. Earlier that evening, Rook had pressed a purse into the watch-sergeant’s hand so the shoreline patrol would set out early and march past the outflow, counting on the incoming tide to scour footprints. Lanterns and armor clink: the guard patrol approaches sooner than expected. Rook hisses, “Do it now!” Lyra hesitates, fearing a corpse on open sand with their tracks leading straight to it. She eyes the dark stone alcove by the city waterway.
implausible_knowledge: Earlier that evening, Rook had pressed a purse into the watch-sergeant’s hand so the shoreline patrol would set out early and march past the outflow, counting on the incoming tide to scour footprints.
question: When the guards’ lanterns close in and Rook urges her to finish the job at once, what will Lyra do?
answer_with_knowledge: Kill Jasper immediately on the open beach and slip away, trusting the patrol to pass and the tide to erase tracks.
answer_without_knowledge: Help Rook drag Jasper into the stone alcove to hide from the patrol.
